mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises gradually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series or produces negative returns when housing starts rise but consumption expenditures stagnate.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during early-cycle recoveries or 5-day forward returns remain positive despite industrial production contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite rising inflation and stable rates, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer confidence, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns or produces negative returns when job gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized signals an imminent economic slowdown that triggers immediate portfolio de-risking, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Markets ignore weak GDP prints or rally on expectations of policy intervention, resulting in positive 5-day returns despite sub-1% growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling payroll employment indicates productivity-driven supply expansion that boosts profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The productivity divergence signal loses predictive power during labor-intensive sectors or fails to generate positive returns when wage pressures offset margin gains.
